comments.author: @WhereonEarth4K
comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Guyana episode. Tours, books, cookbooks, travel resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE GUYANA COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we'd recommend for this episode → https://amzn.to/4owiM21

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN GUYANA*
  ◦ Kaieteur Falls flights, rainforest lodges, and river tours → https://shorturl.at/GnIim
  ◦ Browse all Guyana experiences → https://shorturl.at/FYOAI

*📖 PLAN YOUR GUYANA TRIP*
  ◦ Guyana (Bradt Travel Guide), the only dedicated guidebook to the country → https://amzn.to/4vezPb7
  ◦ Dictionary of Caribbean English Usage, a Guyanese linguist's record of the region's English → https://amzn.to/3QPVZ4F

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO GUYANA*
  ◦ A History of the Guyanese Working People by Walter Rodney, the landmark account of the country after emancipation → https://amzn.to/4eo035n
  ◦ The Ventriloquist's Tale by Pauline Melville, the Whitbread winning novel set in Guyana's Rupununi savanna → https://amzn.to/4xzVlc8

*🍲 BRING GUYANA HOME*
  ◦ Tastes Like Home by Cynthia Nelson, a Guyanese cook's collection of pepperpot, roti, and Caribbean classics → https://amzn.to/4eMmW2e
  ◦ India Tree Demerara Sugar, the large grained cane sugar that takes its name from Guyana's Demerara coast → https://amzn.to/4fQURIa

This will eventually collapse because the current government has failed to solve critical problems that will backfire on their and their elite crooks. I will just laugh at them. LOL!!!

comments.text: Who discovered guinana

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Guyana wasn't really "discovered" in the traditional sense — Indigenous peoples lived there for thousands of years before Dutch, then British, colonization began in the 16th-17th centuries.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Worth noting incomes vary a lot depending on sector - oil and gas jobs pay far above the national average, while most other work hasn't caught up yet, so both things can be true depending on who you ask.

comments.author: @ShaheezaMajid
comments.text: The name of the freedom fighter was KOFI. The slave masters pronounced it as Cuffy

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thank you for that — Cuffy (Kofi) led one of the earliest and largest slave rebellions in the Americas, and it's exactly the kind of detail that deserves wider recognition. Appreciate you adding the name.

comments.author: @sebastianwrites
comments.text: Guyana is rated 74 out of a 100.... so it's a quite healthy democracy, so why is the weslth not being more fairly shared?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Fair question — Guyana's oil wealth is still very new, the boom really only took off around 2020, and institutions built for a much smaller economy haven't fully caught up to the scale of it yet. That gap between production and distribution is a big part of what comes next.

comments.author: @barbaraphillips9896

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: There's real debate in how "poor" gets measured and by whom — colonial-era extraction versus modern GDP tells very different stories. Appreciate you pushing back on the framing.

comments.author: @davidhuttner275
comments.text: This is an interesting video, until it gets to the subject of Jonestown and parrots the official, US Government narrative of what happened there. To get the truth, you must travel to the local Guyanans and win their trust enough for them to confide in you THE MUCH UGLIER BUT MORE PROBABLY TRUTH.

comments.author: @ramdassbissoondyal-xo3pn
comments.text: Is it really the richest country in the world?

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Richest by GDP growth rate over the past decade, not richest overall — Guyana's economy expanded faster than any country on Earth thanks to the oil boom, but total wealth per capita is still catching up.

comments.author: @VibertWhattley
comments.text: Guyana Had Always Been Rich Hope The Guyanese People Benefit Immensely From This Economic Boom

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That hope is the whole story. Thank you for watching. 🌿

comments.author: @NeilBaptiste-r6y
comments.text: So why are citizen complaining about cost of living

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: It's a common pattern in fast oil-boom economies — prices for housing, food, and imports often rise faster than wages can catch up, even while the overall GDP numbers look great.

comments.author: @nana59ization
comments.text:
Some of the statements in this documentary are not true.  Neglect is not a word that should be used to describe Kaietur.  It is remote fall, and covered with dense forest and only accessible by air.
Oil has only been  drilling for 10 years now  as a result, Guyana did not have the financial resources for numerous development projects which are still lacking.  Guyana is a developing country.  It has a long way to go in spite of the oil boom.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Really appreciate the precision here — "neglect" undersells how remote and inaccessible Kaieteur actually is, and you're right that the oil boom is only about a decade old against a much longer development gap. We'll revisit that wording.
